currentState.summary: The public dashboard, private recommendation flow, GitHub Pages deployment, and Raspberry Pi-based scheduled publishing are all working. The product is now focused on making the sync path dependable, especially around weekly rollover behavior and operator visibility.

completedFoundation: Public dashboard for standings, weekly comparison, and golfer availability is live.
Private recommendations page shares the same generated data pipeline as the public dashboard.
GitHub Pages deployment and static JSON publishing workflow are in place.
The Raspberry Pi runner can sync, commit, and push updates on schedule without relying on the Mac being on.
Recovery logic now protects the season view from being wiped when Splash only returns current-week data.

currentPhase.name: Hardening / Reliability
currentPhase.summary: Make the current product dependable enough that weekly refreshes and publishing do not require rescue work.
items: Keep the Pi runner clean and aligned with the main branch so scheduled runs stay rebase-safe.
Reduce the chance of season-history regressions when upstream data is partial or current-week-only.
Lock the Thursday-to-Sunday weekly rollover rules so the dashboard opens the new week on Thursday morning but only books earnings into season totals after the Sunday final refresh.
Make operational checks simple enough that a screenshot or short log snippet is enough to diagnose most failures.

nextPhase.name: Usability
nextPhase.summary: Make the product easier to understand and easier to operate without needing to inspect raw files.
items: Add clearer health and freshness messaging for last sync result, source state, and publishing status.
Improve recommendation transparency so weekly pick guidance is easier to trust and explain.
Polish small UX details like favicon, metadata, and navigation clarity across public and private views.

laterPhase.name: Feature Expansion
laterPhase.summary: Add higher-value league intelligence only after the sync and publishing foundation is stable.
items: Broaden projection inputs and field-aware recommendation logic for tougher weekly pick decisions.
Add deeper season storytelling, team race views, and smarter comparison tools.
Introduce lightweight alerts or summaries for failed refreshes and notable standings changes.

buildTimeline.title: Foundation Complete
buildTimeline.summary: Built the live dashboard, private recommendation workflow, hosted deployment, and shared JSON data pipeline.

buildTimeline.title: Always-On Publishing
buildTimeline.summary: Moved scheduled refreshes onto the Raspberry Pi so updates can run even when the Mac is off.

buildTimeline.title: Season History Recovery
buildTimeline.summary: Fixed weekly refresh regressions so empty future events do not appear as active picks, duplicate tournaments are collapsed, and the prior completed event rolls into season totals correctly.

buildTimeline.title: Weekly Rollover Rules
buildTimeline.summary: Defined the week handoff so Thursday morning opens the new tournament in the week-by-week view, while Sunday night is what finalizes that tournament into season totals and last week's earnings.

buildTimeline.title: Reliability Hardening
buildTimeline.summary: Stabilizing sync behavior, preserving season history during partial upstream responses, and reducing manual recovery work.

buildTimeline.title: Operator-Friendly Product
buildTimeline.summary: Add clearer status, easier monitoring, and more understandable guidance for weekly decisions.

buildTimeline.title: League Intelligence Expansion
buildTimeline.summary: Expand projections, alerts, and deeper season analysis after the automation is consistently dependable.
